title: lepidaploa chalybaea
intro: Lepidaploa chalybaea, also known as the "Blue Violet", is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Lepidaploa chalybaea are said to include wound healing, treatment of skin conditions such as acne and eczema, and as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Lepidaploa chalybaea can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used topically to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
